metadata.name: SimpleFileReceive
metadata.description: Receive XML from FILE adapter, transform via BizTalk map, write to FILE adapter. The Hello World of BizTalk migration.

biztalkInput.orchestrationXml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-16"?>
<!--
  BizTalk Orchestration (ODX) — Simple File Receive
  ===================================================
  Shapes: Receive (activating, FILE adapter) → Construct/Transform → Send (FILE adapter)
  Pattern: Linear — no branching, no correlation, no error handling
  Complexity: Simple

  ODX files are stored in BizTalk projects as XML documents with the om: namespace.
  The orchestration designer in Visual Studio reads and writes this format.
-->
<om:Module xmlns:om="http://schemas.microsoft.com/BizTalk/2003/DesignerData">
  <om:Property Name="InitialCodeFile" Value="SimpleFileReceive.odx"/>
  <om:Property Name="InitialNamespace" Value="SimpleFileReceive"/>
  <om:Property Name="InitialTypeName" Value="SimpleFileOrchestration"/>
  <om:Property Name="Notes" Value="Receives XML order from file share, transforms to processed order, writes output file"/>

  <!-- Service = the orchestration type -->
  <om:Service Name="SimpleFileOrchestration">
    <om:Property Name="Type" Value="Public"/>
    <om:Property Name="Compensation" Value="Default"/>
    <om:Property Name="Transaction" Value="None"/>

    <!--
      Message declarations
      Messages are typed instances of XML schemas (or .NET classes).
      Each message declared here is a variable available throughout the orchestration.
      Migration: These map to Logic Apps workflow variables or body() references.
    -->
    <om:MessageDeclaration Name="IncomingOrder" Type="SimpleFileReceive.Schemas.OrderMessage"/>
    <om:MessageDeclaration Name="OutgoingOrder" Type="SimpleFileReceive.Schemas.ProcessedOrderMessage"/>

    <!--
      Port declarations
      Ports define the communication channels (send/receive endpoints).
      Migration: Receive port → Trigger; Send port → Action (HTTP/Blob/ServiceBus)
    -->
    <om:PortDeclaration Name="ReceiveOrderPort"
                        Type="SimpleFileReceive.PortTypes.IReceiveOrder"
                        Modifier="Private"
                        Polarity="Implements">
      <!--
        Implements = this orchestration IS the service (port binds inbound message TO this orch)
        Polarity="Implements" + activating Receive → generates an activation subscription in MessageBox
      -->
    </om:PortDeclaration>

    <om:PortDeclaration Name="SendOrderPort"
                        Type="SimpleFileReceive.PortTypes.ISendProcessedOrder"
                        Modifier="Private"
                        Polarity="Uses">
      <!-- Uses = this orchestration calls an external service (sends message OUT) -->
    </om:PortDeclaration>

    <!--
      Orchestration Body — the shape graph
      Shapes are rendered visually in the BizTalk orchestration designer.
      Migration engine parses these shapes to build the IntegrationIntent.
    -->
    <om:Body>
      <om:Shape Name="BeginOrchestration" Type="BodyShape">
        <!--
          RECEIVE SHAPE (activating)
          - ActivatesCorrelation="true" → this Receive creates a new orchestration instance
          - Without activation, a Receive would route to an existing running instance
          - Migration → Logic Apps: this becomes the TRIGGER
          - The trigger type is determined by the adapter on the bound receive location (from BindingInfo.xml)
          - FILE adapter → polling trigger (Azure Blob or FileSystem connector)
        -->
        <om:Shape Name="ReceiveOrder" Type="ReceiveShape">
          <om:Property Name="ActivatesCorrelation" Value="true"/>
          <om:Property Name="Message" Value="IncomingOrder"/>
          <om:Property Name="Notes" Value="Activating receive — creates orchestration instance when file arrives"/>
          <om:Port Name="ReceiveOrderPort"/>
        </om:Shape>

        <!--
          CONSTRUCT MESSAGE SHAPE
          Wraps message construction actions (Transform or MessageAssignment).
          Migration: this scope becomes a Transform action in Logic Apps.
        -->
        <om:Shape Name="BuildProcessedOrder" Type="ConstructShape">
          <om:Property Name="Messages" Value="OutgoingOrder"/>
          <om:Property Name="Notes" Value="Construct the outgoing ProcessedOrder message"/>

          <!--
            TRANSFORM SHAPE (inside Construct)
            References a BTM map class — the compiled map transforms IncomingOrder → OutgoingOrder.
            Migration: this becomes a Transform XML action or Xslt action pointing to a converted map.
          -->
          <om:Shape Name="ApplyOrderMap" Type="TransformShape">
            <om:Property Name="ClassName" Value="SimpleFileReceive.Maps.OrderToProcessedOrderMap"/>
            <om:Property Name="Source Message" Value="IncomingOrder"/>
            <om:Property Name="Destination Message" Value="OutgoingOrder"/>
            <om:Property Name="Notes" Value="Applies BTM map: Order → ProcessedOrder (adds ProcessedDate, LineTotals, OrderTotal)"/>
          </om:Shape>
        </om:Shape>

        <!--
          SEND SHAPE
          Sends the constructed message out via the bound send port.
          Migration → Logic Apps: this becomes an action (Blob create, HTTP POST, etc.)
          based on the adapter type on the bound send port (from BindingInfo.xml).
        -->
        <om:Shape Name="WriteProcessedOrder" Type="SendShape">
          <om:Property Name="Message" Value="OutgoingOrder"/>
          <om:Property Name="Notes" Value="Write ProcessedOrder to output file via FILE send port"/>
          <om:Port Name="SendOrderPort"/>
        </om:Shape>

      </om:Shape>
    </om:Body>

  </om:Service>
</om:Module>
biztalkInput.bindingXml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<!--
  BizTalk Binding File — Simple File Receive
  ==========================================
  Binding files connect orchestration ports to physical transports.
  They are exported from BizTalk Admin Console or BizTalk Deployment Framework (BTDF).

  MIGRATION KEY INFORMATION in this file:
  - Adapter type (FILE, HTTP, WCF, etc.) → determines Logic Apps connector type
  - Address (C:\Input\*.xml) → becomes blob container path or trigger config
  - TransportTypeData → contains adapter-specific settings (poll interval, file mask, etc.)
  - Pipeline names → determine if content needs special processing
  - Filter conditions → determine subscription/routing logic (may become Logic Apps Conditions)
-->
<BindingInfo xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
  <Description>Simple File Receive-Transform-Send Bindings — TEST ENVIRONMENT</Description>
  <Timestamp>2026-02-23T00:00:00</Timestamp>
  <Version>3.5.1.0</Version>
  <BindingParameters>
    <GacDeploymentEnabled>false</GacDeploymentEnabled>
  </BindingParameters>

  <ModuleRefCollection>
    <ModuleRef DisplayName="SimpleFileReceive(1.0.0.0)" Name="SimpleFileReceive">
      <ServiceRefCollection>
        <ServiceRef Name="SimpleFileOrchestration" FullName="SimpleFileReceive.SimpleFileOrchestration" Type="1">
          <Ports>
            <!--
              Receive port binding: connects orchestration's ReceiveOrderPort to the physical receive location.
              Migration note: The adapter type (FILE) and address (C:\Input\*.xml) → Blob trigger config.
            -->
            <Port Name="ReceiveOrderPort" PortModifier="2">
              <ReceivePortRef Name="RP_ReceiveOrder_FILE"/>
            </Port>
            <!--
              Send port binding: connects orchestration's SendOrderPort to the physical send port.
              Migration note: FILE adapter, C:\Output\ → Blob Storage create action.
            -->
            <Port Name="SendOrderPort" PortModifier="1">
              <SendPortRef Name="SP_SendProcessedOrder_FILE"/>
            </Port>
          </Ports>
        </ServiceRef>
      </ServiceRefCollection>
    </ModuleRef>
  </ModuleRefCollection>

  <SendPortCollection>
    <SendPort Name="SP_SendProcessedOrder_FILE"
              IsStatic="true"
              IsTwoWay="false"
              BindingOption="0">

      <PrimaryTransport>
        <!--
          FILE adapter send configuration.
          Migration → Logic Apps:
            Address: "C:\Output\%SourceFileName%" → Blob container "orders-outbound" + filename from trigger
            %SourceFileName% macro → @{triggerBody()?['Name']} in Logic Apps WDL expression
        -->
        <Address>C:\Output\%SourceFileName%</Address>
        <TransportType Name="FILE" Capabilities="11" ConfigurationClsid="5e49e3a6-b4fc-4077-b44c-22f34a242fdb"/>
        <TransportTypeData>
          <![CDATA[<CustomProps><CopyMode vt="19">0</CopyMode><AllowCacheOnWrite vt="11">0</AllowCacheOnWrite><UseTempFileOnWrite vt="11">-1</UseTempFileOnWrite></CustomProps>]]>
        </TransportTypeData>
        <RetryCount>3</RetryCount>
        <RetryInterval>5</RetryInterval>
        <ServiceWindow>
          <FromTime>00:00:00</FromTime>
          <ToTime>23:59:59</ToTime>
          <Enabled>false</Enabled>
        </ServiceWindow>
      </PrimaryTransport>

      <SendPipelineName>Microsoft.BizTalk.DefaultPipelines.XMLTransmit, Microsoft.BizTalk.DefaultPipelines, Version=3.0.1.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=31bf3856ad364e35</SendPipelineName>
      <Description>Sends processed XML order to output file location</Description>

      <!--
        Send port filter (subscription).
        This filter determines which messages this send port receives from the MessageBox.
        Migration note: In a purely orchestration-bound send port, the filter may be empty
        (the orchestration explicitly sends to this port). Filters matter more for standalone
        send ports (content-based routing without orchestrations).
      -->
      <Filter>
        <Group>
          <Statement Property="BTS.SPName" Operator="0" Value="SP_SendProcessedOrder_FILE"/>
        </Group>
      </Filter>

    </SendPort>
  </SendPortCollection>

  <ReceivePortCollection>
    <ReceivePort Name="RP_ReceiveOrder_FILE"
                 IsTwoWay="false">
      <Description>Receives XML order files from the file system</Description>
      <ReceiveLocationCollection>
        <ReceiveLocation Name="RL_ReceiveOrder_FILE">
          <!--
            FILE adapter receive configuration.
            Migration → Logic Apps:
              Address: "C:\Input\*.xml" → Blob trigger on container "orders-inbound" with *.xml filter
              PollingInterval (60000ms = 60s) → Recurrence trigger interval: 1 minute
              FileMask: "*.xml" → blob filter pattern
          -->
          <Address>C:\Input\*.xml</Address>
          <TransportType Name="FILE" Capabilities="11" ConfigurationClsid="5e49e3a6-b4fc-4077-b44c-22f34a242fdb"/>
          <TransportTypeData>
            <![CDATA[<CustomProps>
              <BatchSize vt="3">20</BatchSize>
              <BatchSizeInBytes vt="19">102400</BatchSizeInBytes>
              <FileNetFailRetryCount vt="19">5</FileNetFailRetryCount>
              <FileNetFailRetryInt vt="19">5</FileNetFailRetryInt>
              <PollingInterval vt="3">60000</PollingInterval>
              <FileMask vt="8">*.xml</FileMask>
              <RenameReceivedFiles vt="11">-1</RenameReceivedFiles>
            </CustomProps>]]>
          </TransportTypeData>
          <ReceivePipelineName>Microsoft.BizTalk.DefaultPipelines.XMLReceive, Microsoft.BizTalk.DefaultPipelines, Version=3.0.1.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=31bf3856ad364e35</ReceivePipelineName>
          <Description>Polls C:\Input for *.xml files every 60 seconds</Description>
          <Enable>true</Enable>
          <ScheduleTimeZone>UTC</ScheduleTimeZone>
        </ReceiveLocation>
      </ReceiveLocationCollection>
    </ReceivePort>
  </ReceivePortCollection>

</BindingInfo>

trigger.source: Azure Blob Storage (orders-inbound container, polling every 1 minute)

steps.description: Transform incoming Order XML to ProcessedOrder format using BizTalk map OrderToProcessedOrderMap
Write processed order to output blob container (orders-outbound), preserving original filename
